  2. Classes and Interfaces

Package model

package model
The model package defines the core data structures and entities used throughout the application.

These classes represent the main objects in the system—such as users, questions, answers, messages, and reviews— and serve as the bridge between the database layer and the application logic. Each class typically encapsulates relevant fields, constructors, and getter/setter methods for managing data integrity and enabling easy interaction with the databasePart1 package.

Class Summary:

  • Admin – Represents an administrative user with system management privileges.
  • Answer – Defines a single answer associated with a specific question.
  • Answers – Provides utility or collection-level management for multiple Answer objects.
  • Clarification – Represents a user-submitted clarification or suggestion related to a question or answer.
  • invalid reference 
    FollowUpQ
    – Represents a follow-up question tied to an existing question for further discussion.
  • Instructor – Represents an instructor-type user with permissions for reviewing or managing content.
  • Messages – Encapsulates private message data exchanged between users.
  • NavigationBar – Defines elements of the application's navigational UI structure.
  • Question – Represents an individual question posted by a user, including title, description, and metadata.
  • Questions – Handles operations and collections of Question objects (e.g., loading, sorting, filtering).
  • Review – Represents a single review entity, often linked to a user or post evaluation.
  • Reviewer – Defines a reviewer-type user who provides feedback or assessments on questions or answers.
  • Reviews – Manages collections of Review objects, such as retrieving or storing multiple reviews.
  • Student – Represents a standard student user within the system.
  • User – The base user class defining shared attributes and behaviors for all user roles (student, instructor, admin, etc.).

Together, these classes form the foundation of the system’s domain model, ensuring a consistent and organized representation of data. They are used by higher-level components in the logic and pages packages to implement business logic and UI behavior.

  • Class
    Description
    Admin
    Represents an administrative user with full privileged access in the system.
    AdminAction
    Represents an action taken by an admin on an AdminRequest.
    AdminRequest
    Represents a request from instructors to admins for admin-specific actions.
    AdminRequest.Category
    Request categories available to users
    Answer
    Represents a single answer posted to a question.
    Answers
    The Answers class handles all logic related to managing answers, including creation, editing, deletion, and searching.
    Clarification
     
    ContentType
     
    Edits
     
    Instructor
    The Instructor class represents a Instructor entity in the system.
    Messages
     
    ModerationFlag
    Represents a staff-created moderation flag on a question or answer.
    ModerationNote
    Represents an internal note attached to a moderation flag.
    NavigationBar
     
    Notification
    Represents a notification in the system.
    Notification.Type
    Notification types
    Question
    Represents a user-posted question with metadata such as author, title, description, timestamp, tags, and resolution status.
    Questions
     
    Review
    Represents a single review attached to an answer.
    Reviewer
    Represents a reviewer user with permissions to provide feedback and evaluate other users' submissions in the system.
    Reviews
    In-memory manager for Review objects with backing persistence via DatabaseHelper.
    Staff
     
    StaffRequest
    Represents a request from staff members for various actions (e.g., account management, permissions, system changes)
    Student
    Represents a student user with access to questions, answers, and reviews features.
    User
    Representes a generic user in the system, encapsulating common attributes such as ID, username, password, role, name, and email.
    User.Role
